learnerValue: Accurately tests the causal link between work done, internal energy and possible temperature increase.
Tests both specified factors affecting liquid pressure with distinct misconceptions.
Clearly distinguishes conservation of total momentum from unchanged individual momentum or zero final momentum.
Directly tests the specified normal human hearing range using plausible boundary errors.
Efficiently compares both wave type and liquid transmission for P-waves and S-waves.
Tests the three quantities represented by Fleming’s left-hand rule and diagnoses common substitutions.
Tests the two necessary conditions behind the rotational effect in an electric motor.
Distinguishes conversion direction from the named motor-effect mechanism.
Tests that the induced magnetic field opposes either form of original change specified by AQA.
Separates the conditions for inducing potential difference from the complete-circuit condition for current.
Clearly tests the specified alternator–dynamo distinction with plausible output confusions.
Distinguishes microphone conversion direction and generator-effect mechanism from loudspeaker misconceptions.
Tests both the basic transformer structure and the specified reason for using an iron core.
